title: Anthropic’s Code with Claude showed off coding’s future—whether you like it or not
optimized_headline: Anthropic’s Code with Claude: A Glimpse into the Future of Coding
raw_excerpt: The vibes were strong at Code with Claude, Anthropic’s two-day event for software developers in London that kicked off on May 19, the same day as Google’s I/O in Palo Alto. (A coincidence, not a flex, Anthropic staffers assured me.) “Who here has shipped a pull request in the last week that was completely written…
raw_body: The vibes were strong at Code with Claude, Anthropic’s two-day event for software developers in London that kicked off on May 19, the same day as Google’s I/O in Palo Alto. (A coincidence, not a flex, Anthropic staffers assured me.) “Who here has shipped a pull request in the last week that was completely written…
speedrun: Anthropic recently held a two-day event called Code with Claude in London, coinciding with Google’s I/O. This gathering focused on the future of coding, showcasing how AI can assist developers. A notable moment was when attendees shared their experiences with AI-generated code, indicating a shift in how software is created. This matters now as it reflects growing acceptance of AI tools in the developer community, which could reshape coding practices.
why_it_matters: Developers are increasingly using AI tools like Claude, which could streamline their workflows and enhance productivity. This immediate shift indicates a growing reliance on AI in everyday coding tasks.
The event highlights a broader trend where AI is becoming integral in software development, suggesting that companies may need to adapt to these tools to stay competitive in the market.

lenses.eli12: Anthropic's event showed how AI can help programmers write code more efficiently. Imagine having a smart assistant that helps you complete tasks faster. This is important for everyday people because it could lead to better software and faster updates, improving apps we use daily.
lenses.pm: For product managers, the rise of AI coding tools like Claude could address user needs for faster development cycles. This efficiency might lower costs and improve product quality. It’s essential to consider how integrating AI can enhance team capabilities and reduce time to market.
lenses.engineer: From a technical perspective, the event demonstrated how Claude can generate code snippets, potentially improving coding speed and accuracy. Developers shared experiences of using AI-generated code, which could lead to more efficient debugging and testing processes. However, it’s important to consider the implications of AI-generated code on code quality and maintainability.
